title: Ophrys samia
taxonomy: Ophrys samia is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of bee orchid, which is a common name for some species within the genus Ophrys. The genus Ophrys is a group of orchids that are known for their unique and often deceptive flowers, which are designed to mimic the appearance and scent of female insects to attract male pollinators. As a member of this genus, Ophrys samia has adapted a similar strategy to ensure successful pollination. The classification of Ophrys samia is further supported by its botanical name, which is a combination of its genus name and a specific epithet that distinguishes it from other species within the same genus.
